<script>
        // Simple scroll spy for navigation
        window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
            const sections = document.querySelectorAll('section');
            const navLinks = document.querySelectorAll('nav a');
            
            let current = '';
            sections.forEach(section => {
                const sectionTop = section.offsetTop;
                if (pageYOffset >= sectionTop - 150) {
                    current = section.getAttribute('id');
                }
            });

            navLinks.forEach(link => {
                link.classList.remove('text-primary', 'font-bold', 'border-b-2', 'border-primary', 'pb-1');
                link.classList.add('text-on-secondary-container', 'font-medium');
                if (link.getAttribute('href').includes(current) && current !== '') {
                    link.classList.add('text-primary', 'font-bold', 'border-b-2', 'border-primary', 'pb-1');
                    link.classList.remove('text-on-secondary-container', 'font-medium');
                }
            });
        });
    </script>
</body></html>

Use browser_lock before longer automation on an existing tab, then browser_lock with action \\\"unlock\\\" when finished. 5. Use browser_snapshot for accessibility context and browser_take_screenshot for visual verification. 6. Use browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill, browser_select_option, browser_press_key, browser_scroll, and browser_drag for page interactions. 7. Use browser_highlight and browser_get_bounding_box for visual grounding and coordinate diagnostics. 8. Use browser_cdp for page inspection, profiling, runtime evaluation, DOM/CSS queries, and performance data. AVOID RABBIT HOLES: 1. Do not repeat the same failing action more than once without new evidence such as a fresh snapshot, a different ref, a changed page state, or a clear new hypothesis. 2. IMPORTANT: If four attempts fail or progress stalls, stop acting and report what you observed, what blocked progress, and the most likely next step. 3. Prefer gathering evidence over brute force. If the page is confusing, use browser_snapshot, browser_take_screenshot, or CDP inspection before trying more actions. 4. If you encounter a blocker such as login, passkey/manual user interaction, permissions, captchas, destructive confirmations, missing data, or an unexpected state, stop and report it instead of improvising repeated actions. 5. Do not get stuck in wait-action-wait loops. Every retry should be justified by so

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Blank PDF pages are likely caused by `opacity: 0` and `z-index: -1` on the pdf-root element.

The `print-only` class applies `display: none !important`, which overrides inline styles and keeps the root element hidden from html2canvas.

The fix removes the `print-only` class from `#pdf-root` during generation. The element will be positioned off-screen without `opacity: 0`. A direct html2canvas clone may work better than the page-by-page html2pdf approach.

The element will be forced visible with `visibility: visible` and `display: block` rather than relying on `opacity: 1` alone. Millimeter units in styles may cause issues, so pixel equivalents around 794×1123 pixels are preferable. A `print` stylesheet or `window.print` could also be used instead of html2canvas.

External portrait images may taint the canvas and break `toDataURL`, so they should be skipped or replaced with placeholders. The first page path using `.toPdf().get("pdf")` is probably blank too, since it shares the same hidden root element. The entire PDF generation logic will be rewritten cleanly.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The CSS update did not resolve the blank PDF pages. The HTML still contains print-only styles and the old downloadPdf function with opacity set to zero.

html2canvas cannot capture elements with `visibility: hidden`, which explains the blank pages. The solution is to absolutely position the element off-screen at `left: -9999px` while keeping `visibility: visible`, `opacity: 1`, and `display: block`.

The element will be 794px wide. I will replace the custom downloadPdf logic with the full html2pdf library and its built-in pagebreak support. The PDF root markup and CSS will be simplified to keep the element fully visible off-screen.

The downloadPdf function will be rewritten to inject the PDF content directly into the root element, then immediately apply inline styles to render it at full opacity and visibility. A brief pause follows before html2pdf begins processing.

The html2pdf configuration was abandoned because jsPDF pixel units are unreliable. The revised plan is to capture each page with html2canvas while the element remains visible on screen beneath the overlay.

The element will be fixed at the top-left corner with a z-index of 50, sitting just below the overlay’s z-100 layer so the user does not see it flash. The visibility:hidden rule will be removed entirely. Because html2canvas is not exposed globally, the downloadPdf function will rely solely on the html2pdf API. The function will begin by fetching the overlay, root, and button elements.

The footer button is also disabled during the download. The root element is cleared and rebuilt with `buildPdfDocument()`. It is then forced on-screen under the overlay with `z-index: 50`, `pointer-events: none`, and full visibility so html2canvas can paint it. A double `requestAnimationFrame` delay ensures the browser finishes rendering before capturing.

A 200 ms pause follows the double animation frame. The root element is queried for `.pdf-page` elements. Each page is processed sequentially through html2pdf. The first page is captured into a jsPDF instance using A4 millimeter units and a 2x canvas scale.

Each remaining page is converted to a canvas, then added to the jsPDF instance as a new page. The completed PDF is saved, and the root element is cleared afterward. If any step fails, the error is logged and the browser falls back to native printing.

The cleanup block removes the capturing class, clears the root element’s inline styles, hides the overlay, and re-enables both buttons. The buildPdfDocument helper should drop its inline A4 dimensions and rely solely on the `.pdf-page` CSS class. The avatar container needs `line-height` instead of `display: flex` for reliable vertical centering in html2canvas.

The avatar markup will use a 64-pixel square with a 58-pixel line-height and centered text for reliable html2canvas rendering. I will apply smaller, targeted replacements rather than rewriting large sections.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="mcp-cursor-ide-browser-browser_cdp"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **mcp-cursor-ide-browser-browser_cdp**</summary>

Parameters:

```json
{
  "tools": [
    {
      "name": "browser_cdp",
      "parameters": "{\"method\":\"Runtime.evaluate\",\"params\":{\"awaitPromise\":true,\"expression\":\"(async () =\u003e {\\n  if (!window.jspdf || !window.jspdf.jsPDF) return { ok: false, reason: 'jspdf missing' };\\n\\n  // Patch save to capture output instead of downloading\\n  const { jsPDF } = window.jspdf;\\n  const proto = jsPDF.API || jsPDF.prototype;\\n  let captured = null;\\n  const origSave = jsPDF.prototype.save;\\n  jsPDF.prototype.save = function(name) {\\n    captured = {\\n      name: name,\\n      pages: this.getNumberOfPages(),\\n      text: this.getText ? null : null,\\n      output: this.output('arraybuffer')\\n    };\\n    // Also get raw string for text search\\n    captured.raw = this.output('datauristring');\\n    return this;\\n  };\\n\\n  document.getElementById('download-pdf-btn').click();\\n  await new Promise(r =\u003e setTimeout(r, 800));\\n\\n  jsPDF.prototype.save = origSave;\\n\\n  if (!captured) return { ok: false, reason: 'save not called' };\\n\\n  const bytes = new Uint8Array(captured.output);\\n  const latin = new TextDecoder('latin1').decode(bytes);\\n  const header = String.fromCharCode(...bytes.slice(0, 8));\\n  const checks = {\\n    hasName: latin.includes('Alanerdwin') || /Alanerdwin/.test(captured.raw),\\n    hasIshiji: latin.includes('ISHIJI') || latin.includes('Ishiji'),\\n    hasLear: latin.includes('Lear') || latin.includes('Circuit'),\\n    hasPhone: latin.includes('6392') || latin.includes('780'),\\n    hasPage1: latin.includes('PAGE 1') || /PAGE 1/.test(captured.raw),\\n    pages: captured.pages,\\n    size: bytes.length,\\n    header: header\\n  };\\n  // PDF text is often compressed — check uncompressed streams or just size+pages\\n  return { ok: true, checks: checks, compressed: /FlateDecode/.test(latin) };\\n})()\",\"returnByValue\":true}}",
      "serverName": "cursor-ide-browser"
    }
  ]
}
```

{
  "tools": [
    {
      "name": "browser_cdp",
      "parameters": "{\"method\":\"Runtime.evaluate\",\"params\":{\"awaitPromise\":true,\"expression\":\"(async () =\u003e {\\n  if (!window.jspdf || !window.jspdf.jsPDF) {\\n    return { ok: false, reason: 'jspdf missing', jspdf: typeof window.jspdf };\\n  }\\n  const { jsPDF } = window.jspdf;\\n  let result = null;\\n  const origSave = jsPDF.prototype.save;\\n  jsPDF.prototype.save = function(name) {\\n    const ab = this.output('arraybuffer');\\n    const bytes = new Uint8Array(ab);\\n    let b64 = '';\\n    const chunk = 0x8000;\\n    for (let i = 0; i \u003c bytes.length; i += chunk) {\\n      b64 += String.fromCharCode.apply(null, bytes.subarray(i, i + chunk));\\n    }\\n    result = {\\n      name: name,\\n      pages: this.getNumberOfPages(),\\n      size: bytes.length,\\n      header: String.fromCharCode(...bytes.slice(0, 8)),\\n      b64: btoa(b64)\\n    };\\n    return this;\\n  };\\n  document.getElementById('download-pdf-btn').click();\\n  await new Promise(r =\u003e setTimeout(r, 1000));\\n  jsPDF.prototype.save = origSave;\\n  if (!result) return { ok: false, reason: 'save not called' };\\n  // decode and search for uncompressed text operators - jsPDF often embeds text as literal\\n  const bin = atob(result.b64);\\n  const hasAlan = bin.includes('Alanerdwin');\\n  const hasIshiji = bin.includes('ISHIJI');\\n  const hasLear = bin.includes('Lear') || bin.includes('Circuit');\\n  const hasPhone = bin.includes('6392');\\n  const hasEdu = bin.includes('Bohol');\\n  return {\\n    ok: true,\\n    pages: result.pages,\\n    size: result.size,\\n    header: result.header,\\n    hasAlan, hasIshiji, hasLear, hasPhone, hasEdu,\\n    // return small prefix of b64 so we can write file if needed\\n    b64Len: result.b64.length\\n  };\\n})()\",\"returnByValue\":true}}",
      "serverName": "cursor-ide-browser"
    }
  ]
}

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The missing headshot is likely caused by a JPEG transparency issue. jsPDF's addImage converts the circular canvas to JPEG, but JPEG does not support transparency. The transparent pixels outside the circle become black or white during conversion, which can hide the image.

The solution is to export the circular portrait as a PNG instead of JPEG. PNG supports transparency, so the clipped image will render correctly without black or white artifacts.

Filling the canvas with the soft purple background before clipping the circle is another option. This preserves the soft corner color and keeps the file small while still using JPEG. It avoids transparency issues entirely.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixing circular portrait export: JPEG doesn't keep transparency, so I'll fill the canvas to match the sidebar before clipping.

_**Agent (default)**_

The headshot is embedded now.

The PDF was using an **“AS” initials circle** because the remote Google image couldn’t be loaded into jsPDF (CORS). I saved the portrait locally as `assets/portrait.jpg` and the PDF generator draws it as a circular photo.

Hard-refresh (`Ctrl+F5`), then **Download PDF** again — the headshot should appear at the top of the left column.
